semanticCore: Urging, prompting, inciting, advocating, low land, or nudge
quranicRelevance: Not urging one another to feed the poor (Yahuddu - 'la yahuddu 'ala ta'ami', 'la tahad-doona').

keyDerivations.meaning: To urge, prompt, incite, advocate, or encourage
To urge one another, encourage mutually, or collaborate in urging

figurativeOrRareUsages: كَلَّا ۖ بَلْ لَا تُكْرِمُونَ الْيَتِيمَ ﴿١٧﴾ وَلَا تَحَاضُّونَ عَلَىٰ طَعَامِ الْمِسْكِينِ (89:17-18): Nay! But you do not honor the orphan, nor do you urge one another on the feeding of the poor.
summaryEn: The root denotes active urging (Haddh), condemning social negligence where elites neither feed the poor themselves nor encourage (Yahuddu) others to do so.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الحث والدفع المتبادل للفعل (حضّ)، معاتباً وموبخاً كفار قريش لعدم حثهم بعضهم البعض على كفاية وإطعام الفقراء والمساكين.
rawText: تحاضّوا 6 They excited, &c., one another. (S, K.) In the Kur ubi suprà, some read وَلَا تَحَاضُّونَ (Fr, S, TA) Nor do ye excite one another. (Fr.) حاضّهُ 3 , (TK,) inf. n. مُحَاضَّةٌ, (S, K,) He excited him, &c., as above, being excited, &c., by him. (S, K, TK.) In the Kur ubi suprà, among the various readings are these two: وَلَا يُحَاضُّونَ and ولا تُحَاضُّونَ, meaning, accord. to Fr, Nor are they, or ye, mindful. (TA.) حضّضهُ 2 , inf. n. تَحْضِيضٌ: see 1, in two places. حَضِيضٌ A depressed piece (قَرَار) of ground at the place where a mountain ends; (S;) or a depressed piece (قرار) of ground at, or by, the سَفْح [i. e. foot, or bottom, or lowest part,] of a mountain; or in, or at, the lower, or lowest, part thereof, (فِى أَسْفَلِهِ,) the سفح being behind the حضيض; the حضيض being in the part next the سفح, and the سفح being دُونَ ذٰلِكَ [which seems to be virtually a repetition, signifying behind that part, or it may mean above that part]: (TA:) or i. q. سَفْحٌ: (K in art. سفح:) or a depressed piece (قرار) of ground: (A:) or a tract (حِجزٌ [in the CK, erroneously, حَجَر]), and a depressed piece (قرار), in land or ground: (K:) and any low piece (سَافِلٌ) of land or ground: (TA:) and the ground [in an absolute sense]: (S:) pl. [of pauc.] أَحِضَّةٌ and [of mult.] حُضُضٌ. (K.) It has the last of the above-mentioned significations in a certain trad., in which it is related that a present being brought to Mohammad, he, not finding anything upon which to put it, said, ضَعْهُ بِالحَضِيضِ, meaning بِالأَرْضِ; [i. e. Put thou it upon the ground;] adding, for I am only a servant; I eat as the servant eateth. (S.) حُضٌّ , said to be a subst.: see 1. حِضِّيضَى and حُضِّيضَى, said to be substs.: see 1.
